question: Who initially brought Nehemiah word about Jerusalem (Neh 1:2)?
options: Shelemiah, the son of Jozadak the priest

question: Besides Jerusalem what did Hanani inform Nehemiah about (Neh 1:2)?
options: The Gihon spring had been stopped up
The exiles had been attacked by their enemies
The remnant who survived the exile

question: Why were the exiles in great trouble and disgrace (Neh 1:3)?
options: The walls of Jerusalem were broken down
The temple had been burned to the ground
The people were worshipping the gods of the land

question: One what condition would God regarter his exiled people (Neh 1:9)?
options: If they rebuilt the temple and walls of Jerusalem
If they returned to God and obeyed his commands
If they put away their foreign gods and followed God alone

question: What did Nehemiah take and give to the Persian King Artaxerxes (Neh 2:1)?
What did Artaxerxes ask Nehemiah about (Neh 2:2)?
options: How the exiles in Jerusalem were doing
What group of people he belonged to
Why Israel had not paid their tribute to him

question: What did Nehemiah tell Artaxerxes he wanted to do (Neh 2:5)?
options: Restore the gates of the city
Build the temple the the Most High

question: Why did the king grant Nehemiah’s requests (Neh 2:8)?
options: Because the king looked with favor on Nehemiah
Because the king was seeking the favor of the God of Israel
Because the gracious hand of God was upon him
Because God moved the heart of King Artaxerxes

question: Tobiah, one who opposed Nehemiah was from what background (Neh 2:10)?
When did Nehemiah initially survey the broken down walls of Jerusalem (Neh 2:13)?
At night what did Nehemiah survey around Jerusalem (Neh 2:13)?
options: The walls that had been broken down

question: At night what did Nehemiah survey around Jerusalem (Neh 2:13)?
options: The gates that had been burned

question: What gate did Eliashib the high priest and his fellow priests rebuild (Neh 3:1)?
What did the sons of Hassenaah rebuild (Neh 3:3)?
Uzziel son of Harhaiah was original a(n) _________ (Neh 3:8)?
What was Hananiah prior to his work on the repairs of Jerusalem (Neh 3:8)?
Rephiah played what function in Jerusalem (Neh 3:9)?
options: Ruler of a half-district of Jerusalem

question: What repairs did Jedaiah son of Harumaph make (Neh 3:10)?
Hasshub son of Pahath-Moab repaired what tower (Neh 3:11)?
The ruler of the district of Beth Hakkerem rebuilt what gate (Neh 3:14)?
Baruch zealously repaired from the angle to __________ (Neh 3:20)?
options: The house of Eliashib the high priest
The house of Nergal the Persian governor

question: Binnui repaired from his house to the _______ and the corner (Neh 3:24)?
What was near the upper palace (Neh 3:25)?
Shemiah son of Shecaniah was the guard of what gate (Neh 3:29)?
The house of the temple servants and merchants was opposite what gate (Neh 3:31)?
Up to what gate did the goldsmiths and merchants make repairs (Neh 3:32)?
What did Nehemiah pray God would not do for Sanballat and Tobiah (Neh 4:5)?
options: Protect them from the armies of Artaxerxes

question: How high was the wall built by the people with all their heart (Neh 4:6)?
options: All the wall was half height
The wall was completed from the Water Gate to the Sheep Gate
The first course of stones were laid

question: When did Sanballat and Tobiah plot to fight against Jerusalem (Neh 4:7)?
options: When they heard the foundations of the walls were completed
When they heard the gates had been rebuilt
When they heard that the gaps were being closed
When they heard the Water Gate was completed

question: What happened to the plot to attack by Sanballat and Tobiah (Neh 4:15)?
options: King Artaxerxes sent troops to defend the workers

question: How did the workers carrying material respond the the plot of Sanballat (Neh 4:17)?
options: They put the materials on cart to transport it to Jerusalem
They used their spears to carry the materials
They worked with one hand with the other on their weapon
They worked in shifts some working and others on guard

question: Who did Nehemiah say would fight for them as they built the wall (Neh 4:20)?
options: Eliashib and the warriors of Benjamin

question: What were the people to do when they heard the sound of the trumpet (Neh 4:20)?
Why were the people mortgaging their fields (Neh 5:3)?
options: Because the wealthy were buying them up
Because they had no homes to live in
Because they needed to purchase grain
Because they were outside the walls of Jerusalem

question: What did some of the poor have to do in order to pay the knig’s tax (Neh 5:4f)?
options: Cut down the trees on their land
Sell all their grain to the king
Give up the inheritance of their fathers

question: Why did the poor have to sell their children into slavery (Neh 5:4f)?
options: In order to pay the king’s tax
To pay for guards to protect their fields
Because they had no food to feed them
To stop them from being killed by Sanballat

question: In what year of Artaxerxes what Nehemiah appointed governor of Judah (Neh 5:14)?
What did Nehemiah not acquire for himself (Neh 5:16)?
Why did Nehemiah say God should remember him with favor (Neh 5:19)?
options: For all the suffering he had been through
Because he sought the face of the Lord his God
For all he had done for these people

question: When did Sanballat request a meeting with Nehemiah (Neh 6:2)?
options: When the gates of Jerusalem had been completed
When Nehemiah was sending word back to Artaxerxes
When no gaps were left in the wall of Jerusalem
When Artaxerxes sent more soldiers as reinforcements

question: Why was Sanballat wanting to meet with Nehemiah (Neh 6:2)?
options: He wanted Nehemiah to give a good report to Artaxerxes
He wanted to make a covenant of peace with Nehemiah
He wanted to purchase grain from Nehemiah

question: How many times did Sanballat request a meeting with Nehemiah (Neh 6:4)?
What did Sanballat’s letter accuse Nehemiah of (Neh 6:6)?
options: He was plotting a revolt and make himself king
He had stolen articles out of the temple and sold them
He was building a palace for himself at the people’s expense
He was refusing to pay taxes to the Persians

question: Who did Sanballat claim in his letter that Nehemiah had appointed (Neh 6:7)?
options: Priests and Levites to anoint him as king
Prophets to proclaim “There is a king in Judah”
Gate keepers to stop any Persians from entering Jerusalem
Tax collectors to collect revenue for him rather than Artaxerxes

question: After receiving Sanballat’s letter what did Nehemiah pray (Neh 6:9)?
options: That God would strength his hands
That God would be his rock and his fortress
That God would not listen to Sanballat
That God would forgive his sin

question: What did Nehemiah realize about Shemaiah (Neh 6:12)?
options: He was a bad advisor and his advice was against the law
He was a noble who cared only about himself
He was prophesying because Sanballat had hired him
He was a corrupt priest and had no respect for the ways of the Lord

question: Why did Sanballat hired Shimaiah (Neh 6:12)?
options: To kill Nehemiah and do it without having to defend themselves
To trick Nehemiah into giving himself up on behalf of the people
To stop Nehemiah from rebuilding the temple
To intimidate Nehemiah into committing a sin and discredit him

question: How long did it take Nehemiah to rebuild the walls of Jerusalem (Neh 6:15)?
Why did the surrounding nations loose their confidence (Neh 6:16)?
options: Because they realize Nehemiah had the support of Artaxerxes
Because they realized Nehemiah was a great leader among the people
Because they realized that this work had been done with God’s help
Because they realized the strength of the people

question: Who sent repeated letters trying to intimidate Nehemiah (Neh 6:17)?
Who did Nehemiah put in charge after he finished building the wall (Neh 7:2)?
Why did Nehemiah pick Hananiah to be in charge of Jerusalem (Neh 7:2)?
options: Because he feared God more than most men do
Because he was faithful in all his house
Because he was a man after God’s own heart
Because he was a righteous man who walked in the ways of the Lord

question: What was to be done while the gatekeepers were still on duty (Neh 7:3)?
options: The sheep were to be gathered into the city
All those entering the city were to come in
All foreigners must leave the city
The gates were to be shut and barred

question: Who were to be appointed as guards of Jerusalem (Neh 7:3)?
Who had originally taken the exiles into captivity (Neh 7:6)?
In what month had the Israelites settled in their towns (Neh 7:73)
Who did the assembled people tell to bring out the Law of Moses (Neh 8:1)?
When Ezra praised the Lord how did the people respond (Neh 8:6)?
options: They sounded the cymbals and played the stringed instruments
They raised their hands and said “Amen! Amen!”
They sang the song of Moses and the Lamb

question: After saying “Amen” what did the people do before Ezra (Neh 8:6)?
options: They shouted for joy before the Lord that the wall was completed
They bowed down and worshipped with their faces to the ground
They turned their faces toward heaven

question: What had the people been doing while the Law was being read (Neh 8:9)?
On what day of the seventh month did the Israelites confess their sins (Neh 9:1)?
What was noted that the Israelites did just before they confessed their sins (Neh 9:2)?
options: Put always all their pagan idols
Shut the gates of the city and posted guards there

question: How did the confessing exiles spend the first quarter of their day (Neh 9:3)?
options: Standing and reading from the Book of the Law
Bowing with their heads to the ground
Guarding the gates and walls of Jerusalem

question: What did the Levites standing on the stairs do (Neh 9:4)?
options: Tore their robes and confessed their sins
Called with loud voices to the Lord their God
Opened the gates of the temple for all to enter
They bowed their heads and led Israel in prayer to God

question: Whom did the Levites in their prayer say God chose (Neh 9:7)?
Whose heart did the Levites in their prayer say God found faithful (Neh 9:8)?
In the prayer of the Levites what did God see in Egypt (Neh 9:9)?
options: The power and might of Pharaoh
The cities built by the Israelites

question: In the prayer of the Levites what did God send against Pharaoh (Neh 9:10)?
In the prayer of the Levites how did God lead Israel (Neh 9:12)?
In the prayer of the Levites what did God make known at Mount Sinai (Neh 9:14)?
In the prayer of the Levites Moses was identified as _________ (Neh 9:14)?
In the prayer of the Levites where did God get water for them (Neh 9:15)?
In the prayer of the Levites they praise God for being ________ (Neh 9:17)
In the prayer of the Levites they praise God for ________ (Neh 9:17)
options: Being Faithful in all his ways

question: In the prayer of the Levites what did God not do in the desert (Neh 9:19)?
In the prayer of the Levites how long did God sustain them in the desert (Neh 9:21)?
In the prayer of the Levites what two kings were cited as defeated (Neh 9:22)?

Og king of Bashan and Sihon king of Heshbon
Balak king of Moab and Hazael king of Aram
Achish king of Gath and Jabin king of Hazor

question: In the prayer of the Levites what role of the prophets was cited (Neh 9:26)?
options: To admonish the people to turn back to God
To prepare the people to meet God
To speak the word of God
To lead the people in the ways of God

question: In the prayer of the Levites what did God do time after time (Neh 9:28)?
options: Guided them with a pillar of cloud

question: In the prayer of the Levites how did they say a person would live (Neh 9:29)?
options: If he walked in the ways of the Lord
If they returned to the Lord
If they remembered the Lord their God

question: Nehemiah was the son of _______ (Neh 210:1)
Through whom did God give the Law of God (Neh 10:29)?
options: Through the mouths of the prophets
From God written on tablets of stone
Through Moses the servant of God

question: Who was called the “servant of God” (Neh 10:29)?
What did the people promise not to do (Neh 10:30)?
options: Go after other gods and so violate the Law of God
Withhold their tithes and offerings to support the governor
Give their daughters in marriage to the peoples around them

question: What goods did the people say they would refuse to buy or sell (Neh 10:31)?
options: Wine grown by foreigners on their land

question: What was the listed function of the sin offerings for Israel (Neh 10:33)?
What did they cast lots to determine (Neh 10:34)?
options: Who would serve as high priest that year
Who was responsible to be the gate keepers each month
To determine which family would bring wood for the altar
To determine which district should supply the daily burnt offering

question: What was brought to the house of the Lord each year (Neh 10:35)?
The priests were descendants of _______ (Neh 10:38)
What did Nehemiah say should not be neglected (Neh 10:39)?
options: Those who minister before the Lord

question: Who settled in Jerusalem (Neh 11:1)?
How did the people determine who would live in Jerualem (Neh 11:1)?
options: Only certain families were allowed to live there
One had to be of the tribe of Judah

question: How does Nehemiah label Jerusalem (Neh 11:1)?
Over what district of the city was Judah from the tribe of Benjamin (Neh 11:9)?

How lived on the hill of Ophel in Jerusalem (Neh 11:21)?

Whose son was Joiakim (Neh 12:10)?
The heads of the priestly families were listed in whose days (Neh 12:12)?
Talmon was a gatekeeper. What did he guard at the gates (Neh 12:25)?
options: Poor who came to the gates

question: By what title besides priest was Ezra identified with (Neh 12:26)?

options: The first one that went by the Fountain Gate
The second one that went to the Ephraim Gate
The third on that went to the Fish Gate
The fourth one that went to the temple

question: To what wall did the second choir go (Neh 12:38)?

options: The Tower of Hananel and the Tower of the Hundred
The Tower of Asaph and the Tower of the Three Hundred
The Tower of Goliath and the Tower of David

question: The storerooms held all of the following EXCEPT (Neh 12:44)
For whom did did the Levites set aside portions for (Neh 12:47)?

Who was hired to call down a curse on Israel (Neh 13:2)?
Who was Eliashib closely associated with (Neh 13:4)?

options: Spent the tithes and offerings on himself
Gave Tobiah a room in the courts of the house of God

Would not distribute the food supplies to the Levites

question: In what year of Artaxerxes did Nehemiah return to the king (Neh 13:6)?
What did Nehemiah warn the men of Judah of (Neh 13:15)?
options: To close the gates of Jerusalem every night
Caring for the Levites and the priests
Drinking wine when they were in the temple

question: What did Nehemiah see the men of Judah doing on the Sabbath (Neh 13:15)?
What were the men of Tyre doing in Jerusalem (Neh 13:16)?
options: Buying the men of Judah as slaves
Cutting the cedars of Lebanon on the Sabbath

question: What did the children of those who had intermarried not know (Neh 13:24)?
What language did the children of mixed marriages know how to speak (Neh 13:24)?
What oath did Nehemiah make the people take (Neh 13:25)?
options: They would follow in the ways of the Lord
They would not give their daughters to the foreigners’ sons
They would not violate the Sabbath

question: What king of Israel does Nehemiah focus on as sinning (Neh 13:26)?
Who was the high priest during Nehemiah’s days (Neh 13:28)?

Who did Nehemiah drive away from himself (Neh 13:28)?
options: The nephew of Sanballat the Horonite
One of the grandsons of Eliashib the high priest

question: What did Nehemiah make provision for contributions of _______ (Neh 13:31)?
